issues.description: AgentPage.tsx and HistoryPage.tsx are created in the T4 SHA range as 13-line placeholder stubs ('available soon'). Full implementations are T5/T6 scope. Per task note this is observation only, not blocking.

strengths: All 6 agent API functions (createAgentBoard, sendAgentBoardMessage, approveAgentBoard, getAgentBoards, getAgentBoard, getAgentCardOutput) follow the existing request<T> pattern with correct workspace-scoped paths, methods, and bodies (api.ts:230-263)

BoardContext surfaces agent.* SSE events into agentEvents and returns early before void refresh(), correctly avoiding a human-board refetch on every streamed agent.card.token (BoardContext.tsx:403-406)
Routes use the data-router lazy: convention (not React.lazy) and Sidebar adds Agent/History nav links with icons (App.tsx:40-51, Sidebar.tsx:38-39)
AgentEvent client type mirrors the realtime.ts agent.* union exactly; no @anthropic-ai/sdk import, no duplicate EventSource, no fictional SSEContext/useSSE
approveAgentBoard test correctly mocks a 204 with no json() and asserts undefined, matching request<void> behavior; all 15 client API tests pass

issues.description: clearAgentEvents is created inline (() => setAgentEvents([])) so it gets a new identity each render; memoizing with useCallback would stabilize it for consumer effect deps. Carried over from cycle 1, still present.

createAgentBoard and sendAgentBoardMessage tests assert only expect.objectContaining({ method: 'POST' }) and do not verify the request body contains the intent/message. The DELIVERABLE 'POST ... with intent in body' is satisfied by the implementation but is not directly exercised by a test (the plan's TDD spec asserted the JSON body).

AgentCardOutput type declares columnSlug, but the T3 service getCardOutput returns only { output, thinking }. Harmless contract drift; the field is optional in practice and tests mock it. Observation.
